name: pg-browser
description: Full PostgreSQL SQL editor and schema browser — run arbitrary queries, browse schemas/tables/columns, and execute write statements when the plugin is configured out of read-only mode. Connection password is stored encrypted via the platform secret store; statement timeout and row caps gate runaway queries.

configSchema.label: Password
configSchema.description: Stored encrypted via the plugin secret cap (AES-GCM). Never echoed back to the client.

configSchema.label: Database
configSchema.description: Default database to connect to. Switch via `\c other_db` or the schema picker in the UI.

configSchema.label: Read-only mode
configSchema.description: When on, every query runs inside BEGIN READ ONLY and write verbs (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/DROP/CREATE/ALTER/TRUNCATE) are rejected client-side before they hit the wire. Turn off for development databases where you need to CREATE TABLE etc.

configSchema.label: Statement timeout (seconds)
configSchema.description: Hard kill any statement running longer than this. SET LOCAL statement_timeout is applied inside every transaction so concurrent sessions keep their own defaults.

configSchema.label: Max rows per query
configSchema.description: Cap on the result rows returned to the UI. Excess rows are dropped and the response is marked truncated:true so the UI can hint at pagination or stricter WHERE clauses.
